explanation: The requirement says that whenever 'prop_2' holds and 'prop_3' holds, then if 'prop_6' holds, 'prop_4' and 'prop_5' should hold as well, and this is in the context of 'PCV' and 'prop_1'. Assuming 'PCV' is intended to also be an atomic proposition ('prop_0' perhaps), we encode: globally, if PCV and prop_1 and prop_2 and prop_3 and prop_6 hold, then prop_4 and prop_5 must hold at that time.

The requirement is about the possibility (or permissibility) for 'prop_3' (termed as 'prop_4') at prop_1 in prop_0, if both prop_2 and prop_5 hold. In LTL, to express 'it shall be possible to prop_3' under those conditions, we express that: Globally, whenever (prop_0 & prop_1 & prop_2 & prop_5), then it is possible (i.e., eventually) that prop_3 happens. We don't use the name 'prop_4' as a new atomic proposition; it's just a label. So, the LTL formula says: 'Globally, (prop_0 & prop_1 & prop_2 & prop_5) -> (F prop_3)'.

The sentence states that if prop_0 holds, at a point when both prop_1 and prop_2 hold, then it must be possible to perform prop_3 (called prop_4) if prop_5 holds. 'Shall be possible' can be captured by 'eventually', so the formula expresses that whenever prop_0 & prop_1 & prop_2 & prop_5 hold, then eventually prop_3 & prop_4 hold.

If prop_0 holds, and at the time prop_1 holds and if prop_2 holds, then if prop_5 also holds, prop_3 (termed as prop_4) must hold at that point. This can be interpreted that whenever all of prop_0, prop_1, prop_2, and prop_5 are true, then prop_3 (and by term, prop_4) must also be true. Since we must only use atomic propositions explicitly, we conjoin prop_3 and prop_4 if needed. The implication must hold always, so this is a global requirement.

The requirement is that prop_0 and prop_1 hold (without specifying temporal qualification, so interpreted as always holding if at all times). Furthermore, if prop_2 holds, then whenever prop_5 holds, it must be possible for prop_3 to eventually occur (i.e., prop_3 is possible in the future whenever prop_2 and prop_5 occur). The phrase 'termed prop_4' is treated as a label/reference to the property, modeled simply as prop_4 being equivalent to prop_3. To capture that, we can write prop_4 <-> prop_3. The overall LTL binds these together.

explanation: The sentence indicates that when 'prop_0' holds, at the point where 'prop_1' occurs and if 'prop_2' holds, then 'prop_3' (termed as 'prop_4') must happen if 'it is prop_5' (i.e., if 'prop_5' holds at that point). This is interpreted as: Whenever 'prop_0' holds, at the time when both 'prop_1' and 'prop_2' are true, if 'prop_5' also holds, then 'prop_3' (which we note as 'prop_3' itself, with the label being for reference) must hold as well. This can be written by requiring globally that, at all times, if 'prop_0' and 'prop_1' and 'prop_2' and 'prop_5' hold, then 'prop_3' holds.

This requirement states that if the system is in PCV and PSV mode (indicated by prop_0) and some condition (prop_1) holds, then it shall be possible to perform an action (prop_2, the Recruitment Maneuver) if another condition (prop_3) holds. The 'shall be possible' clause in LTL is usually modeled as 'eventually possible', i.e., if the conditions are met, then eventually the action can occur. To model 'it shall be possible to prop_2 if prop_3', we use: if prop_3 holds, eventually prop_2 holds. Combined with the first part, the property holds globally: whenever (prop_0 & prop_1 & prop_3) holds, eventually prop_2 holds.

The sentence requires that globally prop_0 and prop_1 hold. If prop_2 holds, then prop_3 must hold. 'termed prop_4, if it is prop_5' likely means that if prop_5 holds, then prop_4 holds as well (i.e., prop_5 -> prop_4).

This is interpreted so that if prop_2 and prop_4 both hold, prop_3 shall eventually be possible (i.e., will eventually occur).

explanation: The sentence says: 'In prop_0, at the prop_1 and if prop_2, it shall be possible to prop_3, prop_4, if prop_5.' Interpreting this: if at any moment both prop_0 and prop_1 and prop_2 and prop_5 hold, it should be possible (i.e., it is possible eventually) that both prop_3 and prop_4 hold. For 'shall be possible', we can use the 'F' (eventually) operator. For the condition, we put it as a precondition for the possibility. So, globally, if prop_0 & prop_1 & prop_2 & prop_5 then eventually (prop_3 & prop_4).
